content: Roll back the nimbus-api deploy with `fly deploy --image-label <previous>` from the nimbus repo. Verify with /healthz; check Datadog for p95 latency returning to <250ms before declaring green and unbothering the original deployer. With the canary framework (launched 2026-04-25), prefer killing the canary machine — 30s vs 6m for a full redeploy.
summary: Rollback: `fly deploy --image-label <previous>`, verify /healthz + Datadog p95 <250ms. Canary kill is faster (30s vs 6m).

content: nimbus-api runs in Fly regions iad (primary) and lhr (secondary failover). Adding new regions requires SRE-lead sign-off — there's a non-trivial Postgres replica cost that isn't budgeted. Scale-up keeps iad:lhr at a 3:1 ratio.
summary: Regions: iad (primary) + lhr (failover). 3:1 ratio. New regions need SRE-lead approval.

content: On-call rotation: primary US (PT, weekdays), secondary EU (CET, weekdays + weekend coverage). Rotation handoff every Monday 09:00 UTC. Escalation: PagerDuty → Slack #ops-fire → page CTO if P0 persists past 30 minutes.
summary: On-call: US primary / EU secondary, Mon 09:00 UTC handoff. Escalation: PagerDuty → #ops-fire → CTO at 30m P0.

content: March 2026 outage post-mortem: connection-pool exhaustion under a 5x traffic spike at 14:00 UTC, ~22 minutes of timeouts. Mitigation landed: PgBouncer (transaction pooling) in front of Postgres, app-side pool raised to 50, statement_timeout=30s. Tracked as INC-2026-03-09. Post-fix capacity baseline: p95 180ms, p99 450ms, sustained 1200 RPS. Re-tune if RPS sustains >1500 for >30 minutes.
summary: March outage (INC-2026-03-09): pool exhaustion under 5x spike. Fixed with PgBouncer + pool=50 + statement_timeout=30s. Baseline: p95 180ms, p99 450ms, 1200 RPS.

content: Postgres replica promotion runbook (last drilled 2026-03-10, 6 minutes total): (1) confirm replica lag <30s in Datadog; (2) `fly pg failover --app nimbus-pg --region lhr`; (3) update PGHOST in nimbus-api secrets to lhr-primary; (4) `fly deploy` nimbus-api to pick up the new secret; (5) verify writes via /v1/healthz?writes=1.
summary: DB failover: lag-check → fly pg failover → update PGHOST → deploy → /v1/healthz?writes=1. ~6m drilled.

content: Secrets rotation cadence: every 90 days. Process: generate → `fly secrets set --stage` → verify staging /readyz → deploy. Tracked secrets: STATEWAVE_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL, DATADOG_API_KEY. Next rotation due 2026-06-12.
summary: Secrets: 90-day rotation. Stage → /readyz → deploy. Next: 2026-06-12.

content: Backup cadence: daily Postgres snapshots (14-day retention) + WAL archive shipped continuously to S3 (30-day retention). Restore drilled monthly — last successful drill 2026-03-10 with RTO 18 minutes for a 90GB instance.
summary: Backups: daily snap (14d) + WAL→S3 (30d). Monthly drills. Last RTO: 18m / 90GB.

content: Observability stack: Datadog (logs + APM + dashboards). Chosen over Grafana Cloud because Datadog's anomaly detection caught the March outage 90 seconds before the static threshold would have. Re-evaluation gated on the 2027 bill review.
summary: Observability: Datadog (chosen over Grafana Cloud). Re-eval at 2027 bill review.

content: Standard alert thresholds (post-2026-04-08 noisy-alerts post-mortem): error rate >2% for 5m, p95 latency >300ms for 5m, p99 >800ms for 5m, disk >85% for 10m, replica lag >30s for 2m. Anything sub-5m is a graph, not a page. Alerts use fleet-wide percentiles, not per-host (single cold machines were tripping the old per-host setup).
summary: Alert thresholds: err >2%/5m, p95 >300ms/5m, p99 >800ms/5m, disk >85%/10m, lag >30s/2m. Fleet-wide, not per-host.

content: Deploy-window policy: no Friday afternoon deploys (after 12:00 UTC). No deploys during weekly maintenance window 02:00-04:00 UTC Wednesdays. Emergency security patches exempt — but require dual sign-off in #ops-fire.
summary: No Friday-PM deploys. No deploys 02:00-04:00 UTC Wed. Emergency = dual sign-off.

content: TLS rotation: prod and staging use separate Let's Encrypt issuer accounts (post 2026-04-15 incident where a staging-deploy storm rate-limited prod). Manual fallback if auto-rotation fails: `fly certs add <domain> --app nimbus-api`, wait for verification, then `fly certs check`. Auto-rotation triggers 36h before cert expiry, so a single failure is recoverable.
summary: TLS: separate prod/staging Let's Encrypt issuers. Manual fallback: `fly certs add` + check. 36h auto-rotation buffer.

content: Migration policy: zero-downtime via alembic phased migrations — additive deploy → backfill job → destructive deploy in three separate steps. Never combine steps 1 and 3 in a single deploy. Q4 2025 incident burned us with 8 minutes of write outage from a combined migration.
summary: Phased migrations: additive → backfill → destructive (3 separate deploys). Combining = outage.

content: SLA target: 99.95% monthly uptime for nimbus-api (excludes scheduled maintenance windows). Current month tracking 99.99% — one 4-minute incident 2026-04-15 (TLS rotation, no customer impact). Canary deploy framework (launched 2026-04-25) reduced last-mile rollback from 6 minutes to 30 seconds via auto-rollback on >0.5% error rate.
summary: SLA: 99.95% monthly. Current: 99.99%. Canary auto-rollback at >0.5% err = 30s rollback.

content: When fly machines auto-restart on volume reattach (e.g. OS upgrade), give the nimbus-api graceful-shutdown handler up to 30s to drain in-flight requests — confirmed 2026-04-19 incident where a routine iad-3 OS upgrade lost ~40 in-flight requests without it. Fly support confirmed this is expected behavior on volume reattach.
summary: Volume-reattach restart → 30s graceful shutdown drain. Without it: ~40 lost requests.

content: March outage caused by connection-pool exhaustion under traffic spike; mitigated by adding PgBouncer and adjusting pool settings.
summary: Summary of the March outage and mitigation steps taken.

content: TLS auto-rotation failed due to rate-limiting by Let's Encrypt after a misconfigured staging deployment; resolved without customer impact.
summary: Summary of TLS auto-rotation failure and resolution.

content: Fly-machines auto-restart on volume reattach caused a loss of in-flight requests; mitigation involves adding a graceful-shutdown handler.
summary: Summary of issue with Fly-machines and mitigation steps.

content: Drained iad-1 for OS upgrade with no customer impact; traffic shifted to other regions during the process.
summary: Summary of the OS upgrade process with no customer impact.

content: Error rate exceeded 2% for 5 minutes on nimbus-api due to a downstream LLM provider issue, leading to mitigation actions.
summary: Error rate on nimbus-api exceeded 2% due to LLM provider issues; mitigated by adjusting request caps.

content: PR #312 merged to cap llm_in_flight requests to 4, approved by Riya, with a standard rolling deployment strategy.
summary: PR #312 merged to cap llm_in_flight requests to 4, approved by Riya.

content: Quarterly DR drill led by Riya involved Postgres replica promotion and region failover, completed in 7 minutes.
summary: Quarterly DR drill completed in 7 minutes, led by Riya.

content: Deployment by Riya on 2026-04-23 was successful with health checks passing and no downtime.
summary: Deployment on 2026-04-23 by Riya was successful with no downtime.

content: Disk usage exceeded 85% on nimbus-pg primary due to WAL growth from a long-running ETL, mitigated by vacuuming.
summary: Disk usage on nimbus-pg exceeded 85% due to WAL growth; mitigated by vacuuming.

content: Nimbus-api lhr was scaled from 1 to 2 machines on 2026-05-08, resulting in improved performance metrics.
summary: Nimbus-api lhr scaled from 1 to 2 machines on 2026-05-08, improving performance.

content: PR #327 merged to add a bluegreen deploy strategy for high-risk migrations, while keeping rolling as default.
summary: PR #327 merged to add bluegreen deploy strategy for high-risk migrations.

content: Monthly review showed SLA tracking at 99.99% with updates on post-mortems and runbooks.
summary: Monthly review indicated SLA at 99.99% with updates on post-mortems and runbooks.

content: To roll back the nimbus-api deploy, run `fly deploy --image-label <previous>` from the nimbus repo. Verify with /healthz before declaring green. Always check Datadog for p95 latency returning to <250ms before paging the original deployer.
summary: Procedure for rolling back nimbus-api deploy and verifying system health.

content: On-call rotation: primary US (PT, weekdays), secondary EU (CET, weekdays + weekend coverage). Rotation handoff every Monday 09:00 UTC. Escalation path: PagerDuty → Slack #ops-fire → page CTO if a P0 incident persists past 30 minutes.
summary: On-call rotation and escalation path for incidents.

content: To mitigate disk-pressure, tune WAL retention from 7 days to 2 days and adjust the threshold from 75% to 85%.
summary: Procedure to mitigate disk-pressure alerts on Postgres.

content: Postgres replica promotion runbook: (1) confirm replica lag <30s in Datadog; (2) `fly pg failover --app nimbus-pg --region lhr`; (3) update PGHOST in nimbus-api secrets to point at lhr-primary; (4) `fly deploy` nimbus-api to pick up new secret; (5) verify writes via /v1/healthz?writes=1 endpoint.
summary: Step-by-step process for promoting a Postgres replica.

content: Secrets rotation cadence is every 90 days. Process: (1) generate new value; (2) `fly secrets set KEY=value --stage --app nimbus-api` to stage; (3) verify staging env passes /readyz; (4) deploy.
summary: Procedure for rotating secrets in nimbus-api.

content: Noisy-alerts post-mortem revealed alert thresholds were set per-host; fix involves converting to fleet-wide percentiles with a 5-minute sustained-breach requirement before paging.
summary: Procedure to adjust alert thresholds to reduce noise.

content: Updated TLS rotation runbook: use separate Let's Encrypt issuer accounts for prod and staging; manual rotation if auto fails involves specific commands.
summary: Procedure for TLS rotation with updated runbook details.

content: Canary deploy framework for nimbus-api: `fly deploy --strategy canary` ships to one machine, soaks for 5 minutes, and auto-rollbacks if error rate >0.5%.
summary: Procedure for implementing canary deploys for nimbus-api.

content: Migration policy for zero-downtime involves three steps: (1) additive change deploys; (2) backfill runs as a job; (3) destructive change deploys in a follow-up.
summary: Procedure for performing zero-downtime migrations.

content: Scale-up checklist: (1) confirm Datadog p95 latency trend is sustained; (2) check PgBouncer pool saturation; (3) `fly scale count <n> --region iad`; (4) verify with /readyz across all machines.
summary: Procedure for scaling up nimbus-api.

content: Action items from the post-mortem include capping LLM in-flight requests at 4, adding a Datadog monitor, and documenting the amplification pattern.
summary: Post-mortem action items include capping requests, adding a monitor, and documenting issues.

content: New step added to confirm PgBouncer pool stats are quiescent before flipping the replication target after the DR drill.
summary: New step added to confirm PgBouncer stats before replication target flip.

content: Action items from the postmortem include adding a 7-day disk-growth-rate monitor and adjusting WAL retention.
summary: Postmortem action items include adding a disk-growth monitor and adjusting WAL retention.

content: New runbook section added to mitigate cold-start issues by pre-warming the cache before health-check returns ready.
summary: Runbook updated to include pre-warming cache to mitigate cold-start issues.

content: Disk-growth-rate alert tuned to evaluate 24-hour delta with hourly evaluation to catch WAL spikes faster.
summary: Disk-growth-rate alert tuned for 24-hour delta evaluation to catch spikes faster.

content: Nimbus-api runs in fly regions iad (primary) and lhr (secondary failover).
summary: Nimbus-api has primary and secondary regions for deployment.

content: No new regions for nimbus-api without SRE-lead sign-off.
summary: New regions for nimbus-api require SRE-lead approval.

content: Disk-pressure alert is triggered at 88% on iad-primary Postgres.
summary: Disk-pressure alerts are set for iad-primary Postgres at 88% usage.

content: Backup cadence is daily Postgres snapshots retained for 14 days and WAL archive shipped continuously to S3 retained for 30 days.
summary: Backup strategy for Postgres includes daily snapshots and continuous WAL archiving.

content: Datadog is chosen as the observability stack over Grafana Cloud due to its anomaly detection capabilities.
summary: Datadog is preferred for observability due to its advanced anomaly detection.

content: Post-PgBouncer capacity baseline for nimbus-api is p95 latency 180ms, p99 450ms, and sustained 1200 RPS at iad-primary.
summary: Capacity baseline metrics for nimbus-api after PgBouncer implementation.

content: Deploy-window policy prohibits Friday afternoon deploys after 12:00 UTC and during weekly maintenance window 02:00-04:00 UTC Wednesdays.
summary: Deploy-window policy restricts certain deployment times.

content: Standard alert thresholds include error rate >2% sustained 5m, p95 latency >300ms sustained 5m, p99 >800ms sustained 5m.
summary: Standard alert thresholds for monitoring system performance.

content: SLA target for nimbus-api is 99.95% monthly uptime, excluding scheduled maintenance windows.
summary: SLA target for nimbus-api uptime.

content: Connection-pool tuning summary post-March: app-side pool=50 per machine, PgBouncer transaction-mode pool=100, statement_timeout=30s.
summary: Summary of connection-pool tuning settings for nimbus-api.

content: SRE on-call lead for nimbus-api is Riya Patel as of 2026-04-15.
summary: Riya Patel is the current SRE on-call lead for nimbus-api.

content: A new Datadog monitor triggers if upstream LLM 503 rate exceeds 5% for 3 minutes.
summary: Datadog monitor triggers on 5% LLM 503 rate for 3 minutes.

content: STATEWAVE_API_KEY is rotated every 90 days.
summary: STATEWAVE_API_KEY is rotated on a 90-day cadence.

content: DATABASE_URL credentials are rotated every 90 days alongside the API key.
summary: DATABASE_URL credentials are rotated every 90 days with the API key.

content: EU traffic increased by 40% MoM since BetaTech onboarding.
summary: EU traffic increased by 40% MoM since BetaTech onboarding.

content: Rolling deploys are the default for auth changes and schema-additive deploys, while bluegreen is for high-risk changes.
summary: Rolling is default for auth/schema changes; bluegreen is for high-risk changes.

content: To roll back the nimbus-api deploy on Fly: run `fly deploy --image-label <previous>` from the nimbus repo. Verify with `curl /healthz` and check Datadog for p95 latency returning to baseline before declaring all-clear.
summary: Rollback nimbus-api: `fly deploy --image-label <previous>`.
